primary_source: Mayer, M. G. (1949). On Closed Shells in Nuclei. Physical Review, 75(12), 1969-1970. DOI: 10.1103/PhysRev.75.1969.
note: a=126=14*9 is the unique nuclear magic number divisible by 9. All three orbit classes are accessible. This is the last confirmed stable neutron shell closure; Pb-208 (Z=82, N=126) is the heaviest known doubly-magic stable nucleus.
